title: Report income, transfers, and dispositions - Prepare tax returns for someone who died - Canada.ca
content:
## Report income, transfers, and dispositions
- Income on the final return and optional T1 returns
- Income on a T3 Trust Income Tax and Information Return

## Income on the final return and optional T1 returns
Complete a T1 Income Tax Package for each final return and optional T1 return you file. If you choose not to file any optional T1 returns, include all income earned up to the date of death in the final return, even if it is received after the date of death.
You may only file an optional T1 return if you have income from eligible sources and time periods to report on that return.
Most of the income amounts to be reported on the returns can be found on tax slips or previous T1 Returns of the person who died. Ensure that you, as a representative of the deceased, have access to the deceased's tax records through Represent a Client.
If the deceased earned foreign income at any time in 2025, see Reporting foreign income and other foreign amounts .
Minimum tax does not apply to a person for the year of death. However, the deceased may have paid this tax in one or more of the 7 years before the year of death. If this is the case, you may be able to deduct part or all of the minimum tax the deceased paid in those years from the tax owing for the year of death. For more information, refer to Line 40427 – Minimum tax carryover .
The table below shows common income types that have special considerations for the final return and optional T1 returns.
Showing 1 to 19 of 19 entries
Old Age Security pension
Box 18 of the T4A(OAS)
Box 20 of the T4A(P)
Other pensions or superannuation
Box 016 of the T4A , box 31 of the T3 , various boxes in the T4RIF
Elected split-pension amount
Employment insurance benefits
Employment insurance maternity and parental benefits and provincial parental insurance plan benefits
Provincial parental insurance amounts from box 36 and maternity or parental EI amounts from Box 37 of the T4E
Investment income, such as dividends and interest
T5 , T4PS , T3 , T5013
Registered Disability Savings Plan (RDSP)
Taxable capital gains on belongings, property, and investments Calculate taxable capital gains and losses for all belongings, property and investments, which are deemed to be disposed, even if they are not sold on the date of death.
Taxable capital gains on belongings, property, and investments
Calculate taxable capital gains and losses for all belongings, property and investments, which are deemed to be disposed, even if they are not sold on the date of death.
Taxable first home savings account (FHSA) income
Taxable FHSA income – other
Other income, including: Income from an RRIF Rollovers from a registered account or specified RDSP payments
Other income, including:
- Rollovers from a registered account or specified RDSP payments
Self-employment income
T5013 or other business records
Workers' compensation benefits
Social assistance payments
Net federal supplements paid
Box 21 of the T4A(OAS)
For more details on line-by-line income in the T1 Income Tax and Benefit Return , refer to All types of income .

## Income on a T3 Trust Income Tax and Information Return
Report any income the estate earned after death in a T3 Trust Income Tax and Information Return (T3 Return). Although T1 and T3 Returns are structured differently and use different line numbers, most types of income that can be reported on a T1 Return can also be reported on a T3 Return.
Find out if you need to file a T3 Return .
The table below shows common income types that have special considerations for the T3 Return.
Showing 1 to 12 of 12 entries
Taxable capital gains on belongings, property, and investments after the date of death
Pension income received after the date of death
Box 016, 018 and 024 of the T4A , Box 22 of the T4RIF , Box 28 of the T4RSP
Investment income, such as dividends and interest earned after the date of death Includes earnings from investments after the date of death that were in a TFSA before the date death .
Investment income, such as dividends and interest earned after the date of death
Includes earnings from investments after the date of death that were in a TFSA before the date death .
T5 , T4PS , T3 , T5013
Business income after the date of death
T2125 , T5013 , as well as other operating statements
Farming or fishing income after the date of death
T2042 , T2121 , as well as other operating statements
Rental income after the date of death
T776 , as well as other rental income statements
AgriInvest Fund 2 payments (for farmers). Refer to the T4013, T3 Trust Guide .
Box 18 of the AGR-1 , Box D of the RL-21
Income or loss from a deemed disposition (generally only applies to a trust that already existed before the date of death). Refer to the T4013, T3 Trust Guide .
Non-deductible or add-back amounts of interest and financing expenses. Refer to the T4013, T3 Trust Guide .
Line 535 of Schedule T3SCH130
Employer death benefit (up to $10,000 may be non-taxable)
CPP or QPP death benefit (if not reported by the recipient)
Box 18 of the T4A(P)
Other types of income received after the date of death. Refer to the T4013, T3 Trust Guide .
For more details on line-by-line income in the T3 Return, refer to the T4013, T3 Trust Guide .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
